extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won the Madrid sprint finish on a processional final stage where overall positions remained unchanged from Stage 17.
tactical_patterns: BENNETT Sam's two-month fatigue from Tour-Vuelta double suggests sprint speed degradation over consecutive Grand Tours worth monitoring.
Madrid circuit finish has concealed finish line until 250m remaining, requiring sprinters to delay positioning compared to traditional straights.
Downtown Madrid laps turn downhill-then-uphill into finish, favoring patience over early positioning despite fast racing pace.

rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: Held 24-second gap over Vuelta despite Stage 17 pressure from CARAPAZ, demonstrating superior recovery after Tour crash
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: Nearly dislodged ROGLIČ on Stage 17 Covatilla despite 24-second deficit, signaling form peak timing capability
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: positive: Stage win on Angliru plus strong Covatilla ride to secure podium reveals breakthrough Grand Tour climbing capacity
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: positive: Stage win plus fourth overall confirms ability to peak strategically within three-week races despite age
rider_signals.MAS Enric: positive: Fifth overall and best young rider despite Movistar team prize focus, suggesting leadership potential being suppressed
rider_signals.DE LA CRUZ David: positive: Breakaway success elevated to seventh overall from lower position, indicating late-race opportunism when GC teams relax
rider_signals.GAUDU David: positive: Stage 17 win while jumping to eighth overall demonstrates ability to combine breakaway tactics with GC climbing
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: neutral: Won Madrid sprint after two-month Tour-Vuelta campaign, durability confirmed but peak sprint speed unknown
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: neutral: Battled to maintain 10th overall on final mountain, suggests declining Grand Tour competitiveness at advanced age
